name: Electrical Engineering Minor (for Computer Engineering majors)

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 20-course pool is a coherent electrical-engineering subset, the circuits/electronics core (ECE 231/232 circuits, ECE 251 digital design, ECE 252 microprocessors, ECE 271 electronics, ECE 294 lab) with EM fields (ECE 361), signals (ECE 333), and upper electives, plus CS 116/CS 332. But requirementGroups is 0 and no plan of study parsed, so which of these 20 courses make up the stated 17 credits, and in what order, cannot be verified from the data. For CmpE majors much of the shared ECE core (ECE 231/232/251/252/271/294) is already taken, so the differentiating value is the analog/fields/signals/power side of EE (ECE 361 fields, ECE 333 signals, ECE 371/375 electronics, ECE 443 renewable energy) rather than the digital courses they already hold.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the specific required and elective courses that constitute the 17-credit minor, with their intended sequence, rather than a flat course pool.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is explicit and consistent (e.g., ECE 232 'ECE 231', ECE 361 'ECE 231, MATH 213 and MATH 222', ECE 431 'ECE 333, or ECE 232 and MATH 337'), but its deepestPrereqChain of 8 is very deep for a 17-credit minor and reflects electives that carry long ladders from the major. The 2 missing references (missingCoursesSample: ECE 291, ECE 394) are lab-alternate placeholders.
dimensions.suggestion: Choose electives whose prerequisite depth fits a 17-credit minor, and resolve the ECE 291/ECE 394 lab-alternate references so no listed course points at an undefined prerequisite.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No minor-level learning outcomes or objectives appear in the provided data; only a course pool and descriptions are present.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a brief statement of the competencies the Electrical Engineering minor certifies (e.g., circuit analysis, electronics, and signals/systems) as explicit outcomes.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: A total is stated (statedTotalCredits 17.0), which is better than the sibling CmpE minors, but requirementGroups is 0 and creditReconcileDelta is null, so the 17 credits cannot be reconciled against a parsed set of required courses. This is a data-consistency caveat rather than a proven imbalance.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the course-by-course credit breakdown that sums to the stated 17 credits so the total is verifiable.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page states 17 credits but provides no parsed plan of study or required-course list, and because this for-Computer-Engineering-majors variant presents course data and metrics identical to the general variant, so the CmpE-specific waivers are not shown, a reader cannot see the required courses or how this variant differs from the other.
dimensions.suggestion: Render the minor as an explicit 17-credit required/elective list and state precisely which courses are waived for this audience so the variant is self-contained.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The pool is a current EE subset that reaches modern electives (renewable energy ECE 443, wireless/SDR ECE 489, data-network protocols ECE 429) on top of the classical circuits/electronics/fields core. For CmpE majors it should emphasize the analog, electromagnetics, power, and communications courses that distinguish EE from the digital/computer core they already have. Because the required subset is not published, contemporary coverage cannot be guaranteed for every student who takes the minor.
dimensions.suggestion: Guarantee at least one modern elective slot (e.g., renewable energy ECE 443 or SDR/communications ECE 489) in the required structure so the minor reliably reflects current EE practice.

descriptionQuality.justification: Sampled descriptions are present and specific (ECE 231 on circuit theory and system analysis, ECE 361 on electrostatics and Coulomb's/Gauss's laws, ECE 333 on signal models and system representations, ECE 489 on Matlab/Simulink and SDR labs). Only minor truncation and the ECE 429 'Pre or' fragment detract.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are explicit with compound and grade conditions (e.g., CS 116 'CS 115 with a grade C or better', ECE 375 '(ECE 271 and ECE 291) or (ECE 271 and ECE 294)', ECE 489 '(ECE 294 and ECE 421) or (ECE 394 and ECE 421)'). Clarity is reduced by depth-8 chains that are heavy for a 17-credit minor and by the dangling ECE 291/ECE 394 lab-alternate references.

missingTopics.rationale: The minor states 17 credits but no parsed required list, so a student cannot see the minimal path through the 20-course pool; specifying the required subset (e.g., ECE 231/232/271 plus one elective) is the first gap.
The pool has signals (ECE 333) and communications electives (ECE 481/489) but no ML; a machine-learning-for-signals elective would modernize the minor's analytical content.
Renewable energy (ECE 443) is available but no power-electronics/converter course; adding one would connect the minor to electrification and EV/grid work.
Microprocessors (ECE 252) sit in the pool without an embedded/IoT elective that ties low-power sensing and connectivity together for modern EE devices.
Device-level courses (ECE 374/375-adjacent) stop short of IC/VLSI design; a VLSI elective would align the minor with semiconductor-sector demand.

emergingTrends.rationale: ECE 443 already gives the minor a renewables entry; emphasizing it would make sustainability a visible strength.
ECE 489 (SDR) and ECE 429 (data-network protocols) position the minor for modern wireless; refreshing them toward 5G/6G keeps it current.
Chip reshoring is driving demand; a VLSI/device elective would let the minor tap this trend.
Data-driven signal processing is reshaping EE; the signals course (ECE 333) is a natural host for an ML module.
EV and grid electrification depend on converters absent from the pool, motivating a power-electronics elective.

topSuggestions: State explicitly which shared ECE core courses CmpE majors have already satisfied and which analog/fields/power courses (ECE 361, ECE 371/375, ECE 443) define this minor; the data currently duplicates the general variant.
Publish the course-by-course breakdown that sums to the stated 17 credits (requirementGroups is 0 and the delta is null).
Select electives whose prerequisite depth suits a 17-credit minor rather than the current depth-8 chains.
Resolve the dangling ECE 291/ECE 394 lab-alternate references so listed courses have defined prerequisites.
Guarantee a modern elective slot (renewable energy ECE 443 or SDR ECE 489) so the minor reliably reflects current EE practice.
